summary: Decompiled a large block of Kirby fighter work, primarily Kirby SpecialHi physics/collision helpers plus smaller SpecialS and SpecialN helpers, and updated Kirby type/prototype headers to support those matches. The PR also made small signature/header cleanup changes in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c, gm_1601.h, and gryorster.c. Evidence for intent is limited: the PR body only says "Work on Kirby," and there were no review comments beyond a maintainer merge note.

key_files.reason: Main decompilation target; replaced many Kirby placeholder comments with implementations for SpecialHi physics/collision functions, SpecialHi helper callbacks, SpecialS enter, and SpecialN helpers.
Updated declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed Fighter_GObj signatures, added callback prototypes, and changed ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04 to accept an f32 argument.
Adjusted Kirby struct fields to signed types and added ftKb_SpecialHiVars to the motion-vars union so new SpecialHi code could access x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, and xC4 fields.
Updated the call to ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04 to pass lstick_x after the helper signature gained an f32 momentum argument.
Added include for melee/gm/gm_17C0.h, likely to satisfy type dependencies exposed by the header graph.
Small cleanup/signature change: grYorster_802022A0 now takes HSD_GObj* and uses empty-body style; grYorster_802022A4 similarly reduced to an empty body.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialHi1_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi2_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi3_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi4_Phys, ftKb_SpecialAirHi1_Phys, ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Phys, ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys, and ftKb_SpecialAirHiEnd_Phys.

Implemented multiple Kirby SpecialHi collision routines, including ground/air variants and end-state collision handling: ftKb_SpecialHi1_Coll, ftKb_SpecialHi2_Coll, ftKb_SpecialHi3_Coll, ftKb_SpecialHi4_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirHi1_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialAirHiEnd_Coll.
Added inline helper ftKirbyDmgInline to restore Kirby damage/death callbacks to ftKb_Init_800EE74C and ftKb_Init_800EE7B8 after SpecialHi state transitions.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialHi_800F331C and ftKb_SpecialHi_800F346C using newly declared specialhi motion variables, cmd_vars[0], HSD_Randi, damage HP setup, and part-visibility toggles.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialS_Enter by clearing cmd_vars[0], changing to motion state 0x17F, calling ftAnim_8006EBA4, and assigning accessory4_cb to fn_800F53AC.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04 as a momentum-stop helper taking an f32 argument and applying it to gr_vel on ground or self_vel.x in air.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A38 as a predicate for Kirby EatWait/EatFall motion IDs.
Updated ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c so inlineB0 passes lstick_x into ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04.
Changed ftKb_Hat.x4 and ftKb_DatAttrs.speciallw_hp from u32 to s32, matching new signed arithmetic/assignment usage.
Added struct ftKb_SpecialHiVars to union ftKb_MotionVars with integer state fields, a Vec3, padding, and a trailing float field.
Added prototypes for fn_800F21E8 and fn_800F53AC, and typed several ftKb_SpecialHi helper prototypes in ftKb_Init.h.
Added include dependencies for ftcliffcommon and lbrefract in ftKb_Init.c, and gm_17C0.h in gm_1601.h.
Changed grYorster_802022A0 from Ground_GObj* to HSD_GObj* and simplified two empty stage functions.
smart_moves: The PR paired function body decompilation with header/type updates, avoiding isolated implementations that would leave callers or prototypes inconsistent.
Changing ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04 to take the input-derived f32 argument and updating its existing caller in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c preserved cross-file API consistency.
Adding ftKb_SpecialHiVars to the motion-vars union allowed the SpecialHi code to use named motion variable storage instead of raw offsets, a reusable decomp pattern for fighter move states.

The SpecialHi collision code reuses common transition helpers such as ftCommon_8007D5D4, ftCommon_8007D7FC, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ft_CheckGroundAndLedge, and ftCliffCommon helpers rather than duplicating ground/ledge behavior.
The inline ftKirbyDmgInline consolidates repeated callback restoration logic used after multiple motion-state changes.
Signed type corrections for ftKb_Hat.x4 and speciallw_hp reflect actual usage in arithmetic/randomization and damage HP assignment, reducing casts or unsigned mismatch artifacts.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter action routines, expect implementation work to require motion-vars union growth; named union members make state-machine code much clearer and easier to match.
Cross-file helper signatures must be updated at both declaration and call sites. Here, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04 needed an f32 argument and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c had to pass lstick_x.
Collision-state functions often differ mainly by ground/air transition helpers and target motion IDs; keeping those parallels visible can help match families of fighter routines.
Callbacks such as death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, accessory4_cb, and part visibility helpers are important matching clues in fighter state transitions.

Small stack-shaping artifacts like PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(5), and PAD_STACK(6) were used in some functions, but comments such as "Todo: Remove This," "Fake," and "Fix" indicate these should be revisited rather than blindly copied.
Signedness in data attributes and fighter vars can matter for matching when fields participate in randomization, counters, or HP assignment.
Empty functions can often be normalized to compact empty-body style once their signatures are known, as seen in grYorster_802022A0 and grYorster_802022A4.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix, e.g. ftKb_SpecialHi1_Phys, ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04.
Move-state routines follow suffixes such as _Phys for physics, _Coll for collision, _IASA for interruptability, and _Enter for state entry.
Unknown-but-addressed helper functions retained address-based names such as ftKb_SpecialHi_800F331C, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F346C, fn_800F21E8, and fn_800F53AC.
Fighter object parameters are consistently typed as Fighter_GObj* gobj in Kirby fighter APIs when known.
Local data-attribute pointers follow existing conventions such as ftCo_DatAttrs* temp_r30 = &fp->co_attrs and ftKb_DatAttrs* dat_attr = fp->dat_attrs.
New motion-vars union member names reflect the move family, e.g. specialhi and specialn_pe, while still using placeholder fields x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14 where semantics are not yet known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK placeholders to shape stack layout in several functions, including ftKb_SpecialHi1_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi2_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi2_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Coll.
Introduced unused local stack variables in air physics functions with comments indicating they were needed for stack matching.
Preserved exact motion-state IDs and flags such as 0x181, 0x184, 0x185, 0x188, 0x17F, and 0xC1082U rather than abstracting them prematurely.
Used callback casts in ftKirbyDmgInline for death2_cb and take_dmg_cb assignments, suggesting type compatibility was not fully expressed in callback typedefs.
Matched ground/air momentum behavior by selecting gr_vel versus self_vel.x based on ground_or_air in ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04.
Used atan2f of floor normal components multiplied by facing_dir to set part rotation through ftParts_8007592C, matching collision-slope adjustment behavior.
Kept several fields semantically unknown but structurally accurate in ftKb_SpecialHiVars, which is preferable to inventing names before evidence is available.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Going to go ahead and merge this. Please create a new PR for additional changes.
review_feedback.lesson: No technical review feedback was recorded in the provided slice; maintainer guidance was only to land this work and continue further changes separately.

follow_up_queries: Check later Kirby PRs for removal or replacement of PAD_STACK comments added in ftKb_SpecialHi routines.
Search for later semantic renames of ftKb_SpecialHiVars fields x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, and xC4.
Look for subsequent PRs typing fn_800F21E8 and fn_800F53AC with descriptive names.
Verify whether ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3570 and nearby SpecialHi helpers were decompiled in follow-up work.
Search for later cleanup of callback typedef casts in ftKirbyDmgInline-style assignments.
